rationale: The "fail · in-game" hook is a proven casual-puzzle UA pattern, but the source archetype is a bus/parking jam concept — its grid-based vehicle routing has no visual or mechanical analog to Marble Sort's physics-drop conveyor sorting, so a direct port would feel deceptive. Palette frictions are also significant: the member palette uses saturated primary RGB tones that clash with the game's soft pastel lavender/orange identity. Recommendation: only adapt the "why is this level so hard, can you win?" framing onto a genuine Marble Sort fail scenario (e.g., wrong-color marble lands in the wrong box on the conveyor) rather than reusing the bus-jam visual.
